3.happening: Count the {len(values)} array slots that describe the tree.
3.why: We need the size before building node objects.
3.invariant: n is the length of the description array.

4.happening: Start an empty list of real tree nodes.
4.why: We build node objects the visualization can draw.
4.invariant: nodes holds every non-null value as a node.

5.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
5.why: The sweep fetches nodes by id in O(1).
5.invariant: by_id[i] is the node stored at slot i.

6.why: It records each node as unseen, queued, or visited — that drives the colors.

7.happening: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
7.invariant: Slots below i already have a state.

9.happening: Start the child-slot walker just past the root.
9.why: Children are handed out in array order from slot 1.
9.invariant: child is the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Loop over every slot to build the nodes.
10.why: Each real value becomes a node linked to its children.
10.invariant: Slots below i are already built.

11.why: A null slot is a placeholder, not a node.

12.happening: Create node {i} with value {values[i]} and empty child links.
12.why: Children are filled in from the following slots.
12.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

13.happening: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
13.why: Children come from the next slots in order.
13.invariant: child points at the candidate left child.

14.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
14.invariant: A null candidate leaves the left child empty.

15.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s left child.
15.why: It is the next real node in level order.
15.invariant: left now points at a real child id.

16.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

17.happening: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
17.why: The right child is the slot after the left.
17.invariant: child points at the candidate right child.

18.happening: Is that candidate slot a real node (not null)?
18.invariant: A null candidate leaves the right child empty.

19.happening: Link slot {child} as node {i}'s right child.
19.why: It is the next real node in level order.
19.invariant: right now points at a real child id.

20.why: Each slot is claimed exactly once.

21.happening: Add the finished node to the tree.
21.why: It now has its value and child links.
21.invariant: nodes grows by one built node.

22.happening: Register node {i} under its id for fast lookup.
22.why: The sweep fetches nodes by id.
22.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

23.why: The queue holds the current frontier of node ids.

24.why: cur becomes a real id once we dequeue.
24.invariant: cur = -1 means no node is being expanded.

25.happening: Start the level counter at zero.
25.why: Each level we finish adds one.

26.happening: The tree is non-empty — seed the sweep.
The tree is empty — no levels to sweep.

27.happening: Enqueue the root id to start the first level.
27.why: The root is the first frontier.
27.invariant: The sweep begins at the root.

28.why: Queued nodes glow amber, waiting to be expanded.
28.invariant: The root waits in the queue.

29.happening: Nodes remain queued — sweep another level.
29.why: Each pass drains one whole level.
29.invariant: The queue holds this level's nodes.

29.happening: The queue is empty — every level has been swept.
29.invariant: depth now equals the tree's height.

30.happening: Snapshot this level's width: {len(queue)} node(s) queued.
30.why: We expand exactly this many before descending a level.
30.invariant: level_size fixes the current level's node count.

31.happening: A new level begins — bump the depth (currently {depth}).
31.why: Every full level adds one to the depth.
31.invariant: depth counts levels started so far.

32.happening: Loop once for each of the {level_size} nodes on this level.
32.why: We expand the whole level before going deeper.
32.invariant: k counts nodes handled on this level.

33.happening: Take the front node off the queue to expand it.
33.why: Breadth-first expands the earliest-queued node next.
33.invariant: The front of the queue is handled first.

34.why: Expanded nodes turn blue and are counted.

35.why: Its children join the next level.
35.invariant: left is the left child id or null.

36.why: Both children may extend the tree deeper.
36.invariant: right is the right child id or null.

37.why: A real child forms part of the next level.
A null child adds nothing to the next level.

38.happening: Enqueue the left child ({left}) for the next level.
38.why: It will be expanded one level deeper.
38.invariant: The queue grows by the left child.

39.why: It now waits its turn in the queue.

40.why: A real child forms part of the next level.
A null child adds nothing to the next level.

41.happening: Enqueue the right child ({right}) for the next level.
41.why: It will be expanded one level deeper.
41.invariant: The queue grows by the right child.

42.why: It now waits its turn in the queue.

43.happening: The level's nodes are all expanded — clear the cursor.
43.why: No node is being expanded between levels.

44.happening: Return the level count as the maximum depth: {depth}.
44.why: The number of levels equals the longest root-to-leaf path.

init.happening: Building the tree and seeding the queue with the root.
init.why: BFS needs the tree and a starting frontier.
init.invariant: Nodes pop in; the root enters the queue.

loop.happening: Sweeping the tree one level at a time.
loop.why: Each pass drains a whole level and queues the next.
loop.invariant: The queue holds the current frontier.

move.happening: Dequeuing the next node to expand.
move.invariant: The front of the queue is handled first.

check.happening: Testing whether a child exists before queueing it.
check.why: Only real children extend the next level.

update.happening: Expanding a node and queueing its children.
update.why: Each level's children form the next frontier.
update.invariant: State and counters track the sweep.

return.happening: Returning the number of levels as the depth.
return.why: The queue is empty; every level is counted.
